summary: Added a running-app smoke runner that checks the runnable Xcode app contract before build, then builds, installs, launches, and captures a non-empty simulator screenshot artifact with structured JSON proof or precise blocked/failed results. Preserved the follow-up proof queue for Write flow verification, screenshot proof, and optional legacy handoff backfill.

contract_status_changes.before: Contract only; agents could report simulator verification without a standardized build/install/launch/screenshot artifact
contract_status_changes.after: Implemented with a runner that emits running-app-smoke only after build, install, launch, and non-empty screenshot succeed

contract_status_changes.before: No structured blocked result for missing runnable app project, scheme, target, bundle ID, simulator, or screenshot artifact
contract_status_changes.after: Blocked and failed smoke results identify the exact blocked_contract or failed_stage and keep proof_level below running-app-smoke

contract_status_changes.before: Running-app smoke was the only immediate proof slice under discussion
contract_status_changes.after: Follow-up queued slices now classify Write flow verification, Write screenshot proof, optional legacy handoff backfill, and a separate Home protocol scheduling slice

residual_risks: The smoke screenshot is an artifact of launch health, not a reviewed UI-flow or screenshot-baseline proof for a specific product screen.
The real smoke was run before committing this slice, so its JSON records the pre-commit checkout as dirty; rerun after commit for release-grade clean provenance.
No physical-device or TestFlight smoke was performed.
Out-of-scope Home protocol schedule edits were present and left unstaged instead of being reverted.
recommended_next_reason: The next useful product proof slice remains write-note-promotion-flow-verification, but it is not queued in this slice.
